items.q: A skill done in a stable, predictable setting is best classed as:
items.why: Students confuse the continua; a closed skill (e.g. a penalty) is unaffected by a changing environment.

items.q: What are open skills mainly affected by?
items.why: Students think all skills are alike; open skills (e.g. a pass in a game) depend on the environment.

items.q: What does 'tactics' mean in practical performance?
items.correct: Plans and decisions used to outwit opponents
items.why: Students confuse tactics with equipment or rules; tactics are deliberate decisions to gain advantage.

items.q: Which is a recognised role within a sporting activity?
items.why: Correct answer: Official (e.g. referee). “Spectator's pet” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
